methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Zephyr Fusion is a pre-seed stage nuclear energy company founded by Galen Burke and Edward Hinson. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across the nuclear energy sector: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private comparables, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers yields fewer than 3 datapoints in each category. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the wide dispersion in development stage and capital requirements across the nuclear energy landscape. No triangulated valuation range can be responsibly derived from the available comparable data.

The primary constraint is the thinness of the pre-seed nuclear energy cohort. A credible valuation would require either: (1) expanding the analytical aperture to include earlier-stage energy technology companies outside the nuclear sector, (2) employing cost-of-capital or discounted cash flow methods if financial projections are available, or (3) deferring valuation until post-seed funding when the company approaches a broader peer set. Without sufficient comparable evidence, quantitative triangulation cannot be supported.
